summary: Soundboard capture of an Anthem‑era February 1968 show with sparkling China Cat Sunflower, tight Cryptical > The Other One and a rare Born‑Crossed, delivering generally excellent audio.
review: This recording documents a standout early‑1970s period when the Dead were shaping the psychedelic sound heard on *Anthem of the Sun*. Reviewers highlight the luminous China Cat Sunflower, an energetic Eleven, a tight Cryptical > The Other One, and the rarity of Born‑Crossed paired with New Potato. The soundboard source provides clear, balanced audio, though some listeners note track‑splitting quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical performance praised by most reviewers and a solid soundboard source; minor criticisms about track editing keep confidence at medium.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No explicit complaints; assumed clear, balanced mix typical of SBD releases. Some listeners object to how The Other One is split into multiple tracks, but audio fidelity itself is not questioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Shows from this February 1968 run flow tightly, with the rare Born‑Crossed/Near‑Potato pairing and a cohesive Cryptical > The Other One sequence that fans of the Anthem era cherish.

band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals praised as strong on this performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen's organ is highlighted for adding vivid color and intensity throughout the jams.
